close_reason: Fixed: mode guards now return InvalidUri instead of Backend
description: When get(), put(), or sign() is called on a wrong-mode backend (e.g. get() on a key URI), the error returned is SecretError::Backend. This is semantically wrong — a mode mismatch is a configuration error (the user passed the wrong URI type), not a backend failure. It should be SecretError::InvalidUri or a dedicated variant. The current classification means callers cannot distinguish 'your URI is for signing, not storage' from 'the TPM returned an error'. This is especially confusing because the inventory factories already reject wrong-mode URIs at construction time, so these runtime checks are only reachable via direct Tpm2Backend::from_uri() + trait method calls, but the error type should still be accurate.
title: Mode errors use Backend variant instead of InvalidUri
